reviews.review_text: I generally liked Dying Light, but it didn't really hit me that hard. I enjoyed the story, there are lots of side missions that are fun to do. The characters exist, some moments are interesting, but I can't say the story was very memorable. I mostly just wanted to run around the city, do tasks and fight zombies rather than follow the plot. I liked the gameplay more. Parkour is really cool: you can run on rooftops, jump between buildings and quickly escape from enemies. Fights are fine, there are plenty of weapons, but sometimes it all gets a bit repetitive. Overall, Dying Light is a good game with a fun story. Playing was interesting, I just can't say it really hit me hard.

Literally & figuratively one of the greatest zombie games I've ever played. It's a horror & thrill ride masterpiece right until the end. The orange glow of Harran will be forever baked into the back of my mind. Techland started out with 'Dead Island' & it's great, but I always felt like it was missing something. 'Dying Light' is the more sophisticated older brother, in which they cooked to perfection, the successor if you will. From time-to-time in the past as cringe as this is... I would just open up this game just to listen to the main menu theme or even go into game stand on a random rooftop & just listen to the ambience. Everything about this is utter brilliance from the story, soundtrack & even the atmosphere. You won't be disappointed, it's pure nostalgia. In the future when people look back on this game it will be considered a classic in the zombie apocalypse genre. Stop what you're doing & play this from start to finish. It's a must! 

Good Night, Good Luck! My Rating: 9.5/10
Really fun game to play with friends. It's intense and I real like the feelings of it
One of the GOATs, and even tho it's already pretty popular, I consider this game underappreciated still.

greatest zombie game ever and there's also a dropkick mechanic play this game
10/10

[h1] The Best Open-World Zombie Experience I've Played [/h1]

Dying Light really strikes a great balance with Action and Horror elements while also giving the player unprecedented movement abilities. What would likely be a boring open world game is massively expanded with the addition of the Day Night Cycle (and its effects on the world) and the parkour system.

Story wise I don't think there is much special here, honestly I found a few of the side quests to be the best content. One that is really memorable involves the search for insulin for a young child, which leads you on a chase across the city searching for it. When I did this it was getting dark in the game but I felt given the circumstances resting would not make sense. This created a race against the clock for me to find it and beat the quest before the volatiles came out. That is what Dying Light does best.

Ultimately, great game and goes on sale quite a lot so grab it when you can.

I love the parkour and combat feels smooth and night time feels actually scary. A very good game even if its more than 10 years old, the zombie designs are fantastic when it comes to special zombies. Overall i loved the game.

If you’re looking for a cooperative experience, this is it. the world is rich with lore, offering a deep campaign alongside tons of rewarding side content. The gameplay loop is deeply satisfying: you get to customize your look with different outfits, scavenge for parts to craft cool weapons, and clear out infested areas.

This game is a home run that will stand the test of time. The Game came out in 2015 and still stands shoulder to shoulder with the today's games both visually (the day and night cycles are done so well in terms of that) and in  terms of quality. The music takes this game to the next level and cements it as a classic. Three distinct sandbox locations (if we include the following DLC) each standing shoulder to shoulder in terms of quality in their own unique ways while still somehow being part of the same game. The ambience music set of the slums is a masterpiece (I believe it is actually a redone set after the 10th anniversary. In any case, it is perfect).

One of the greatest games of all time. Certainly the peak of zombie games.

Absolutely legendary zombie game, basically a classic at this point. 

-Gameplay. One of the best I've ever had an honor to experience. Melee combat is great and juicy, gunplay isn't that great, but it gets a pass. Parkour is outstanding, definitely one of the best there is. Leveling is surprisingly good, most of the perks are great and you really want to level up. Also I really recommend to complete the game solo and in coop. Coop is absolutely great here(Well, it was basically designed with the coop in mind).

-Story it's ok. I don't really care about the cast, they are not that  great, but protagonist and antagonist are actually good and it's interesting to listen to them. Rais(antagonist) actually has a lot of  funny bits of dialogue, so It's worth to play just to listen to them. I want to give cudos to voice actors, cause their work really elevates the experience, especially Crane''s. Side quests are not that special, you will mostly do them for the experience points.

-OST. There aren't any tracks that are really memorable, but they set the atmosphere and do their job, so no complaints here.

-Graphics. A bit outdated, but zombies' art direction is great and atmosphere(especially in the prologue) is outstanding. Beginning of the game is just another experience entirely, because you are actually afraid of zombies and a night. Later you become an overpowered beast, and it's great, but it's another experience.

-Technical Issues. Didn't experience any issues. It runs great, and maybe I've seen a couple of serious bugs, but it usually resolves by a restart.

10/10, masterpiece of a game. Also I want to give my thanks to the developers for still supporting the game after all these years. Buy for the full price with the friend and enjoy.

I played through the entire campaign with a friend, it was fun and a hilarious experience. He threw a bomb at me during a race because I was winning. 

Dressing up as Bill from Left 4 dead and using Gnome Chompski from Half life 2 to kill zombies was not what I expected so it was a nice surprise!

Speaking of Zombies, I was wondering how strong the Volatiles were. I heard about them before I played the game and they're INSANE. 

The first time I managed to kill a Volatile early in game, It took about 6 Gas canisters and 1 Explosive Barrel... and myself. I lit up the gas canister and made sure the Volatile stayed but before I could escape the Volatile caught me at the last second and I just accepted my fate and I died from the explosion, but so did the Volatile.
one of those games i constantly find myself coming back to. Probably one of the best games to come out of the late 2000s and early 2010s zombie FPS genre
Never got to finish it till now, it was fun. Story lacked a bit, but it was satisfying defeating Rais. Definitely one of the better zombie games out there.

Dying Light is one of the greatest games ive played. I bought this game on sale over a year ago with my friend and it was some of the most fun I have had playing with him. No other story game before or since has brought us the same joy and excitement. It feels so odd to say but the music in this game makes me feel so nostalgic. Especially the menu music. Last time i felt nostalgia like this was with watching clips of 2010 roblox or 2015 minecraft on xbox. The story is so much fun and the combat and parkour made this game a blast. It was so much fun being terrified but also there was so much content in a map that isnt that big to todays standards. I would do anything to play this game again for the first time with him. We tried DL2 and it wasnt as good as this game and we stopped playing it. We are planning on buying the beast soon and i hope its as good as dying light 1.

it started with a classic cliché (there's nothing wrong with that) but then the story started to go from ten to five Crane acted like he was there for months and not a few hours/days the story was probably something like free sauce because the developers focused more on what everything is possible to do in the world
constantly respawning zombies made the game annoying the longer you played
too slow collecting loot from zombies even with talent
lock picking has no accelerator after ten or so unlocks on the achiv I've only unlocked if the crate had a purple/orange item inside no talent/item that would make a simple lock instantly and make the difficulty easier for the others
the first larger zombie with a "hammer" when hitting the ground threw players away and also lit spilled barrels of diesel or destroyed barrels/gas cylinders like WTF is this?
the night was useless, even the double xp didn't impress me at all
zombies were more aggressive= great
shifters at every step ?= crap+annoying
it doesn't matter if you kill a shifter or a sprinter silently, the others immediately know they're dead and will run at you = more crap
weapons have to be repaired = no problem
weapons have a limited number of repairs? even on story difficulty? = total crap
Grappling Hook as a talent was way too late
I would have liked to have gotten a parachute there
the second half of the last mission (climbing) + the "fight" with Rais was completely lifeless it looked like the developers just wanted to prolong the ending
the difference in the amount of xp gained for strength and agility was like the difference between day and night, total crap
the challenges were absolutely horrible they didn't have their own layer so if there was a secondary q at the challenge location the NPCs that q spawned there would happily shoot at you from left to right
failing the challenge will take you back to the beginning = great
any injuries sustained during the challenge upon failing the challenge were not healed, not even on story difficulty.... seriously?
after about 3 hours of play I was determined to give it a thumbs down but then I modded the game with a few light mods to improve the quality of life and it was immediately better I understand that the developers want to give players a challenge BUT they can't just reduce the hp and dmg of NPCs with an easier difficulty, the easier difficulty is supposed to give freedom and the harder challenge In the end it got a thumbs up but  only thanks to the mods and DLC weapons

Arguably one of the best video games ever made. Mirror's edge with zombies works so incredibly well. The story is fine enough, but the moment to moment gameplay is where Dying Light really shines. You can play it like an fps, or a platformer, or a movement shooter, or any combination of those really. It doesn't matter if it's singleplayer, multiplayer, your first playthrough or the tenth; the game is just fun. Favorite thing to do is go on top of a building with my brothers, shotguns and lots of ammo, and we'd just stay up there all night blasting zombies. 10/10, DLC is 100% worth it, singleplayer is fun, co-op is better.

I am quite certain that this is _THE_ most immersive & impressive zombie game I have come across till now. It is excellent at parkour; it is phenomenal if you want to play it as a stealth game with simple takedown features, crouching etc. It gives you so much variety in terms of playstyles; you can be cautious/realistic, a gunblazer, a melee lunatic, a parkour pro who rarely touches the ground and sticks to the rooftops; you can choose to play all missions exclusively at night if you like the kick of that challenge.

Story is not as immersive but it suits the playstyle well and allows it to shine whilst also giving a reasonably convincing and actionable narrative that encourages you to play the game from start to finish; and then restart at higher difficulties which also impacts the replayability extremely positively.

The map of harran is the greenest zombie map I have seen in a zombie game so far with exception of dead island 1 and 2 which I am yet to play; it offers a very high degree of verticality which suits its parkour and fast paced gameplay; and is extremely explorable. Lighting, weather and atmosphere suit a post apocalyptic zombie world set in a tropical zone which makes the environment vibrant and not feel dull (though that is just a personal opinion).

Difficulty scaling is pretty good; normal modes are well suited to most players; while the hard modes are made challenging by improving enemy durability and introducing tightened and less forgiving mechanics; but rarely is the gameplay suffocating until and unless you get placed in a difficult situation either by game design or by your own poor choices. Weapon design and degrading takes the cake because it makes every strike feel impactful and precious, as well as representative of weapons at lower durability. Gun gameplay is Okay at best but acts as a good and fun option especially on lower difficulties. 

I talk about this around 2.5 days into gameplay hours and this is when I have not even tried the online mode; or some of the dlc's like the bozak horde, hellraid, or the following dlc.

My only gripe with this game is the astonishing memory leaks over long hours of gameplay, which allows you to play for only 2.5 hours before having to quit and restart the game; however the utility: Intelligent standby cleanup (ISLC) solves that & tweaking nvidia game settings allows me to push for around 3 hours; by which time I generally take breaks myself.

Overall, this is my first pc zombie game which I have played and this is probably the best place for a new player to start playing games of the zombie apocalypse genre.

5/5 score from my end despite some minor jank (which is really due to the age of the game)

Overall, Dying Light is a pretty fun game. I enjoyed playing through it and getting 100 percent completion, but honestly, that is about it for me. 
The game itself is high quality and definitely well made. The parkour feels great, the combat is enjoyable, and the world is fun to explore during the first playthrough. However, once you finish the story and most of the side content, there is not much motivation to keep playing.
That is probably my biggest issue with the game. While the experience is good, it feels a little underwhelming when going for full completion because there is not much that keeps you engaged afterward. It ends up feeling more like a one-and-done experience rather than a game you constantly want to come back to.

I still think it is a good game and worth playing, but personally, it did not leave a huge lasting impression on me after I finished everything.
iam so glad  I bought this game , a wonderful experience especially with friends cant  wait to play the other 2 games

A friend recommended Dying Light to me, so I decided to give it a try.

During the day you slowly get confident, running around and fighting zombies without thinking too much about it. But the second it gets dark, the whole game changes and suddenly you’re sprinting across rooftops trying not to die.

The parkour never gets old either. Running through the city, climbing buildings, and escaping zombies at the last second feels amazing even after hours of playing.

I finished the whole story mode and loved it. The story is what really made the game special for me. The atmosphere, the characters, and the feeling of surviving in a collapsing city made everything feel way more immersive.

The progression is also super satisfying. You start out struggling with broken pipes and weak weapons, then later you feel almost unstoppable.

This was one of my first zombie games and honestly I’m glad it was, because it set the bar really high for the genre.

9/10 - [i]Do you live by your own rules? Or are you merely someone else's puppet.[/i]

This game is a gem, like lightning in a bottle it is so rare to see a project come to fruition with such a distinct and well executed vision. When I was a kid I enjoyed single player exploration based games like Skyrim. Rarely have I felt a game scratch a similar sort of itch. This game, while being a "zombie game" somehow managed to scratch it. Please play it!!!!!!
